# Apprenticeship

Apprenticeships are the gold-standard of “earn and learn” for workers and career seekers
to achieve their career goals with competitive wages.

Apprenticeship opportunities are offered through employers or program sponsors and
apprenticeship courses are limited to registered apprentices.

Follow these steps to become an Apprentice.

STEP 1: Review the program sponsor list and find an occupation you'd like to pursue.

#### Construction Craft Laborer

The Laborers’ apprenticeship program is for those with little or no experience in
the construction trades who want to begin a career as a Construction Craft Laborer.
The Program allows apprentices to perform many of the types of construction performed
by Laborers such as pouring concrete, laying asphalt, installing storm drains and
water mains, working on bridges and driving tunnels. Laborers also perform many safety-related
duties such as flagging and traffic control. Apply through Northern California District
Council of Laborers.

[https://ncdc-laborers.org/](https://ncdc-laborers.org/) or  [https://www.norcaltc.org/become-an-apprentice/](https://www.norcaltc.org/become-an-apprentice/)

#### Marine Service Technician

A Marine Technician is a craftsperson skilled in the building, maintenance and repair
of pleasure boats. This is a 15-month program with the apprentices being paid from
day one of their training. Nine months are spent at the Spaulding Marine Center, in
Sausalito, learning shop safety & tool skills while gaining familiarity to all parts
of a boat including propulsion, marine carpentry, electrical systems and plumbing.
The last six months are spent rotating through a variety of employer boatyards in
the Bay Area hardening skills in the industry. College credit and a certificate is automatically earned with completion of the program.
Apply through the Spaulding Center during open application periods.

[https://www.spauldingcenter.org/](https://www.spauldingcenter.org/)

STEP 2: To apply, contact the program sponsor from the list above and get accepted into their
program.

STEP 3: The program will setup your on-the-job training and classroom instruction.

### For additional programs, visit [Apprenticeship4you.com](https://www.apprenticeship4you.com/)

[Deadlines](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/deadlines.php "Dates and Deadlines") - [BankMobile & Refunds Dates](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/bankmobiledisbursements.php "BankMobile") - [Forms & Petitions](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/forms.php "Forms & Petitions") - [Financial Literacy](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/literacy.php) - [Net Price Calculator](https://misweb.cccco.edu/npc/481/npcalc.htm) - [Policies & Procedures](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/policies.php "Policies & Procedures") - [Resources for Foster Youth](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/fosteryouth.php) - [Resources for Student Parents](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/ab2458.php) - [Resources for Undocumented Students](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/dream.php "Resources for Undocumented Students") - [Resources for Veterans](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/veterans-fa-steps.php "Financial Aid for Veterans") - [Tax Information  (1098-T)](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/tax.php) - [Verify My Application](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/verifymyapplication.php) # California College Promise Grant ### [CCPG Waiver Online Application](https://bog.opencccapply.net/gateway/bog?cccMisCode=481 "CCCPG Fee Waiver") The **California College Promise Grant (CCPG)** helps eligible students by **waiving the $46 per unit enrollment fee** for an academic year (starting with the summer term). This program is **only available to California residents** or students who qualify under **AB 540** regulations. **Important:** If you **do not** complete a **FAFSA** or **California Dream Act Application (CADAA),** you must **apply separately** for the CCPG Fee Waiver. **If you require a paper fee waiver:** - [**Download the 2025-26 CCPG Application PDF (English)**](https://www.chabotcollege.edu/finaid/docs/2025-26-english-ccpg-application-a11y.pdf) - [**Download the 2025-26 CCPG Application PDF (Spanish)**](https://www.chabotcollege.edu/finaid/docs/2025-26-spanish-ccpg-application-a11y.pdf) * * * ### **Eligibility Criteria for the Promise Grant** Students must meet **ONE of the following** eligibility requirements: ## Type A - Public Assistance Recipients Students qualify if they or their family are currently receiving: - **Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF/CalWORKs)** - **General Assistance (GA)** - **Supplemental Security Income/State Supplemental Program (SSI/SSP)** **Other Eligible Groups:** - Dependents eligible for a **CalVet Dependent Fee Waiver** - Dependents of a **Congressional Medal of Honor recipient** - Dependents of a **victim of the September 11, 2001 terrorist attack** - Dependents of **law enforcement or fire suppression personnel killed in the line of duty** **\*Students must submit supporting documentation for verification.** ## Type B - Low-Income Students #### Documentation Required Students qualify based on **income standards** set by the **U.S. Department of Health and Human Services**. - Income verification may require submission of **federal tax returns, W-2s, or other income documents.** - Eligibility is based on **family size and income level** from the **prior year**. - **Income limits are updated annually and are subject to change.** | **Number in Household** | | **2025-2026 waiver**
**\*Maximum Income for the year 2023** | | --- | --- | --- | | **1** | | $22,950 | | **2** | | $30,660 | | **3** | | $38,730 | | **4** | | $46,800 | | **5** | | $54,870 | | **6** | | $62,940 | | **7** | | $71,010 | | **8** | | $79,080 | | \*Maximum Income Standards​ | | Add $8,070 for each additional family member. | ## Type C - Unmet Financial Need Students qualify if they: - **Submit a FAFSA or CADAA** (REQUIRED) - Have an **unmet financial need** of at least **$1,104**, determined by the **Financial Aid Office**. \*The **Expected Family Contribution (EFC)** is subtracted from the **Cost of Attendance** to determine financial need. If the remaining amount is**$1,104 or greater,**the student qualifies for the fee waiver. * * * ### More Information: ## How the CCPG Fee Waiver Works - Once awarded, the fee waiver covers **summer, fall, and spring** terms for the academic year. - **Students must reapply each year** by submitting a **FAFSA or Dream Act Application**. - Fee waivers are automatically **posted to student accounts** once eligibility is confirmed. - If eligible, students **will not be charged** the $46/unit enrollment fee when registering for classes. ## Retroactive Fee Waivers & Refunds - If a student **retroactively qualifies** for a fee waiver, the **college may reimburse previously paid fees**(only within the current academic year). - If a student **becomes ineligible after receiving the CCPG Fee Waiver,** the college may **recover ineligible fees.** - Students who **already paid fees** can apply for a **refund** using the **Application for Refund of Enrollment Fees**, available at: - **Admissions & Records Office (Building 1600, Second Floor)** - **Online on the [FORMS page](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/forms.php)** ## CalVet Fee Waiver Program The **College Tuition Fee Waiver for Veteran Dependents** covers **mandatory system-wide tuition and fees** at: - **California Community Colleges** - **California State Universities (CSU)** - **University of California (UC) campuses** \* **The program does not cover books, parking, or housing costs.** - **More Information & Application:** [CalVet College Fee Waiver](https://www.calvet.ca.gov/VetServices/Pages/College-Fee-Waiver.aspx) - [College Fee Waiver for Veterans' Dependents Programs](https://laspositascollege.edu/veterans/waiverprogram.php) ## Loss of CCPG Fee Waiver Eligibility Students **must** maintain **academic and progress standards** to keep their CCPG Fee Waiver. #### **Academic Standard – Minimum GPA** - **Students must maintain a cumulative GPA of 2.0 or higher.** - A GPA below **2.0 for two consecutive primary terms**(Fall and Spring) may result in the **loss of the fee waiver.** #### **Progress Standard – Minimum Course Completion** - Students must successfully **complete at least 50% of their coursework.** - Completing less than **50% of total attempted units for two consecutive primary terms** may result in the **loss of the fee waiver.** #### **Combination of Academic & Progress Standards** Students may lose eligibility if they: - Have **two consecutive terms** where their **GPA is below 2.0**, and/or - Have **two consecutive terms** where they **complete less than 50% of attempted units.** \* **Students will be notified within 30 days after each primary term if they are placed** **on academic/progress probation.** \* **A second consecutive term of probation may result in the loss of the CCPG Fee Waiver** **at the next registration period.** ## Appeal Process for Loss of CCPG Students may appeal the loss of their fee waiver under **certain circumstances.** #### **Definition of "Consecutive Terms"** - **Consecutive terms** include **two primary semesters**(Fall and Spring) **with no more than ONE primary term of non-attendance in between.** - Example: - **Attended Spring 2023, skipped Fall 2023, attended Spring 2024** → **Consecutive terms** - **Attended Fall 2022, skipped Spring 2023 and Fall 2023, attended Spring 2024** → **Not consecutive** \* **Foster Youth and students eligible for the CalVet Fee Waiver are exempt from this** **policy.** **Financial Aid Office** Federal School Code: **030357** Building 1600, Second Floor Room 1650 (925) 424-1580 Fax (925) 449-6038 [lpcfinaid@laspositascollege.edu](mailto:lpcfinaid@laspositascollege.edu) _\*Please email from your [Zonemail](https://www.laspositascollege.edu/zoneinfo/index.php "Zonemail") & include your **W#**!_ [FAFSA / CADAA Workshop Schedule](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/workshops.php) Office / Window Hours (hours subject to change) Monday 9:00 am – 5:00 pm Tuesday 9:00 am – 7:00 pm Wednesday 9:00 am – 5:00 pm Thursday 9:00 am – 5:00 pm Friday 9:00 am – 1:00 pm **Our after-hours DROPBOX is in the hallway between the Admissions & Financial Aid Offices.** [**Meet Our Financial Aid Staff**](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/staff.php "Meet Our Financial Aid Staff") [**Frequently Asked Questions**](https://www.laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/faq.php "Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)") **Financial Aid TV** [![Financial Aid TV Videos](http://www.laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/assets/images/fatv-ad.jpg)](https://lpc.financialaidtv.com/ "Financial Aid TV Videos") **Financial Aid TV En Español** [![Videos En Español](https://laspositascollege.edu/financialaid/assets/images/AskQuestions-Espanol.PNG)](https://lpc.financialaidtv.com/browse/audience/spanish-speakers "Financial Aid TV Videos")

# Transcripts

## REQUEST OFFICIAL TRANSCRIPTS

Las Positas College provides a third-party online transcript ordering system through
Parchment. Place all official transcript orders through [Parchment](https://www.parchment.com/u/registration/34444/account).

#### REGULAR SERVICE:

- Processing time is _ten_ business days and fees are $5 for each transcript ordered.

#### RUSH SERVICE:

- Processing time is two business days and fees start at $15 for each transcript ordered. _Fees may vary depending on the RUSH service chosen._

## PARCHMENT INFORMATION

Please click on the Parchment Storefront link for additional information, pricing
and the learner support [Parchment website](https://www.credentials-inc.com/CGI-BIN/dvcgitp.pgm?ALUMTRO030357%20). Chabot-Las Positas Community College District produces a district transcript of
courses completed or in progress from both Chabot and Las Positas Colleges.

#### START YOUR ORDER HERE

[https://www.parchment.com/u/registration/34444/account](https://www.parchment.com/u/registration/34444/account)

## FREE TRANSCRIPT REQUESTS

- Las Positas College offers two free transcripts for all students. After a student’s
free services are completed, future transcripts orders are placed through Parchment.
- Free transcripts take ten business days to process and then are sent first class mail
through the U.S. Postal Service. Once the transcript leaves our college, it is the
responsibility of the USPS to deliver. Mailing is not trackable or guaranteed. Lost
transcripts may result in having to request and pay for additional copies.
- All other services and ordering options including RUSH orders and hold for grades
or degrees are only offered through Parchment.
- [Transcript Request Form](https://laspositascollege.edu/admissions/assets/docs/Transcript-Req2021.pdf)
- Submit all transcript orders by email to [lpc-admissions@laspositascollege.edu](mailto:lpc-admissions@laspositascollege.edu)

Pick up service is not available for both Free and Parchment transcript orders.

## UNOFFICIAL TRANSCRIPTS

#### CURRENT STUDENTS

- Students who are currently attending Las Positas College or Chabot College may obtain
unofficial transcripts by logging on to [MyPortal.](https://experience.elluciancloud.com/clpccd)
  - How to view your unofficial transcript via MyPortal:

    1\. Log in to MyPortal

    2\. Locate the "Classic CLASS-Web" card, then click on the "Student Services" link

    3\. "Student Services" tab

    4\. "Student Records" link

    5\. "Unofficial Academic Transcript" link

    6\. Submit button

#### PRIOR STUDENTS

- Students who are not currently attending either Las Positas College or Chabot College
may request their unofficial transcript using the [Unofficial Transcript Request Form](https://laspositascollege.edu/admissions/assets/docs/Request%20for%20Unofficial%20Transcript.pdf).


  - Submit via:

    **email** to: [lpc-admissions@laspositascollege.edu](mailto:lpc-admissions@laspositascollege.edu)

    **OR**

    **mail or drop off** to:

    Las Positas College - Admissions & Records

    Attn: Unofficial Transcript Request

    Building 1600, Room 1670

    3000 Campus Hill Dr

    Livermore, CA 94551
- NOTES:

  - Student's signature and government-issued photo ID (driver's license, passport, etc.)
     is required for release of unofficial transcripts. Under provisions of the [Family Education Rights and Privacy Act of 1974](https://laspositascollege.edu/admissions/privacy.php), transcripts will not be released without written consent of the student.
  - There is no fee when requesting unofficial transcripts
  - Please allow up to 10 business days for processing

# Student Clubs

Welcome to Las Positas College Student Life! Getting involved in Student Life is a
great way to be connected to the campus, make new friends, gain valuable leadership
experience, develop organizational skills, help build community on campus, and have
fun.

**Clubs** **are a way for students to connect with people who share similar interests.**

Las Positas College has numerous clubs encompassing cultural, educational and special
interests. We encourage all students to get involved and find something that they
are interested in!

The [Student Government](https://www.laspositascollege.edu/lpcsg/index.php) welcomes you to learn about and join all the clubs on campus or start a club if the one you're

Our Mission

Las Positas College is an inclusive, learning-centered, equity-focused environment
that offers educational opportunities and support for completion of students' transfer,
degree, and career-technical goals while promoting lifelong learning.

[View/Print PDF](https://laspositascollege.edu/catalog/current/index.pdf)

# College Catalog 2025-2026

## Degrees and Programs

Welcome to Las Positas College! LPC is rated the #1 community college in the Bay Area
and the third-best in all of California! I am excited that you have decided to join
us for the Fall 2025 semester, to pursue your educational goals and career aspirations.

View a list of all degree and certificate programs offered at Las Positas.

![Invest in You.](https://laspositascollege.edu/catalog/_resources/images/investinyou.jpg)

Nothing should stand in the way of your future. That's why financial aid is available
to most students who need it. Get help paying for college costs like tuition and books
to help you reach your educational and career goals.

### Eligibility for Admission

Admission is open to anyone who is 18 years old or older; or a high school graduate
or completed a high school equivalency test, General Education Degree (GED), or California
High School Profi ciency Exam. A high school student currently enrolled in 10th, 11th
or 12th grade may participate in the Concurrent Enrollment Program. Younger students
may enroll through some slect special programs.

Although every effort has been made to assure the accuracy of the information in this
Catalog, students and others who use this Catalog should note that laws, rules, and
policies change from time to time and that these changes may alter the information
contained in this publication. Changes may come in the form of statutes enacted by
the Legislature, rules and policies adopted by the Board of Trustees of the Chabot-Las
Positas Community College District, or by the Chancellor or designee of the institution.

### OTHER RESOURCES

* * *

### New Student Group Planning Session

An abbreviated education plan is one or two semesters in length and is typically used
for entering first time students.

- [New Student Group Planning Session fillable PDF](https://laspositascollege.edu/register/assets/docs/New%20Student%20Group%20Planning%20Session.pdf)

* * *

### DegreeWorks

Degree Works  allows you to view your Student Educational Plan and confirm what courses
you have completed, as well as what courses remain, toward meeting your educational
objective(s). DegreeWorks matches your academic and course history, as well as your
current course schedule, against the degree requirements as defined in the [LPC College Catalog](https://laspositascollege.edu/class-schedule/catalog.php).

- To access Degree Works, follow these steps:

1. Log into [MyPortal](https://experience.elluciancloud.com/clpccd).
   - For help logging into MyPortal, visit the [MyPortal Resources page](https://districtazure.clpccd.org/tech/myportal.php).
2. Locate the "DegreeWorks" card.
3. Click the "Access DegreeWorks Here" link.

* * *

### Assist

The official course transfer and articulation system for California’s public colleges
and universities (CSU & UC). Use the assist website to identify the lower division
courses for your major that should be completed at Las Positas College prior to transferring
to the 4-year university. ·

- To access the assist website, , follow these steps:

1. Visit the [Assist homepage](https://assist.org/)
2. Select Las Positas College as your institution
3. Select your transfer university in the agreements with other institutions box
4. Select view agreements
5. Then select your major. The courses on the right-hand side of the sheet are the major
    courses that you should complete at LPC before you transfer.

We are here to provide you with support as you navigate your Steps to Success. Our
dedicated team is ready to assist you promptly, whether in person, over the phone,
or via email.

[New Student Support Center](https://www.laspositascollege.edu/supportcenter/)

Career Coach

# ESL College Reading and Writing Pathway Certificate of Competency (NCY)

## About the Program

The ESL College Reading and Writing Pathway provides a supportive, noncredit pathway
into transfer level ESL courses. Students have the opportunity to take a sequence
of four courses, from beginning to intermediate, which will focus on reading and writing,
grammar, vocabulary development, and speaking and listening skills. The curriculum
emphasizes rigorous study of the foundational English language skills of reading,
writing, and grammar. Students who successfully complete the pathway will be able
to enroll in UC and CSU transfer level ESL classes, and will be better prepared to
pursue their educational goals, including language learning, transfer, Associate’s
Degree, certificate, or CTE.

## Career Opportunities

Students who complete this pathway are eligible to enroll in transfer level ESL courses
and a variety of other non-ESL classes in order to achieve their educational and career
goals. In any field, the ability to communicate effectively in written and spoken
English is essential, so completing this certificate will open up many career opportunities
for the student.

## Program Outcomes

Upon completion of this program, students are able to:

- Comprehend a variety of authentic reading materials at the intermediate level.
- Matriculate into credit, transfer-level ESL courses.
- Use foundational grammar, including all verb tenses and types of sentences.
- Utilize a variety of study skills and strategies for language acquisition.
- Write paragraphs and essays with control of organization, development and language
at the intermediate level.
